spec_match.rationale: The broken escaped script was replaced with a live browser-find measurement path. The page uses window.find() when Chrome can select the article result, measures the selected range before and after upstream layout shifts, and clearly labels the DOM mirror fallback if native selection is unavailable or outside the article.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised Run browser find, Shift once, the anchoring mode select, a no-match query, Start shifts, and Stop. The status cards updated for the find source, measured movement, and timer state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still demonstrates find-in-page stability under dynamic upstream layout shifts, matching the feature-index description while making manual native-find verification explicit.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces browser-find support, DOM fallback, no-match queries, overflow-anchor:auto stability, overflow-anchor:none movement, reset behavior, rate-controlled timers, and stopped timer state.

references.rationale: The concept page now links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5070370113323008 and the CSS Scroll Anchoring spec.

design_system.rationale: The updated UI uses shared CSS variables, keeps labels associated with controls, uses black text on amber highlights for contrast, and sets text color with all custom button backgrounds.

summary: Pre-fix DevTools MCP reproduced the broken page: the article stage was empty and Chrome reported Uncaught SyntaxError: Unexpected token ')' from HTML-escaped JavaScript. Post-fix testing in Chrome 148 at http://localhost:3000/v139/scroll-anchoring-priority-candidate-fix/find-in-page-stability/ confirmed window.find selected the article result, Shift once in overflow-anchor:auto reported 0.0px movement with 232.0px scroll adjustment, the opt-out branch reported 232.0px movement with 0.0px adjustment, a no-match query reported no match with zero markers, and Start/Stop updated the timer state. Console was clean and local page/style requests returned 200. The feature conformance route reported 6 PASS, 0 FAIL.
